rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rents [Ccp 708.510] And Appointment Of Receiver [Ccp 708.620 Et Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2024, Line 1. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WEISSBERG's Motion For Assignment Of Rents [Ccp 708.510] And Appointment Of Receiver [Ccp 708.620 Et Seq.]. Plaintiff Weissberg's motion for assignment of rents and appointment of receiver is denied without prejudice as moot. Defendant Peinado's most recent declaration says he and his family are now residing at the property and there are no longer any tenants. (Peinado Decl., par. 2.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4 (California Tort Claims Act)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2024, Line 10. PETITIONER REGINA QUARLES' Hearing On Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4 (California Tort Claims Act). Plaintiff's petition for "an order for relief from the provisions of Government Code 945.4," a claim presentation law, is granted. Plaintiff is an AC Transit bus driver injured in a collision with a Golden Gate bus on April 5, 2023, giving her sixth months to present a claim to Golden Gate - a deadline she missed. On December 29, 2023, plaintiff submitted a request for presentation of a late claim, which Golden Gate denied on February 29, 2024. This petition was then timely filed. (Govt. Code 946.6(c).) Plaintiff says her "failure to present the timely claim was due to mistake and excusable neglect," so she should be relieved. (Id.; Pet. 5:10-11.) She says the accident was investigated by law enforcement, AC Transit and Golden Gate. She filled out claim forms and believed worker's compensation was her sole remedy. A lawyer she consulted did not disabuse her of this notion or tell her of the six-month claim deadline that she can recall. She learned she might have a claim against Golden Gate when she consulted a second lawyer in December 2023. This "court has broad discretion in ruling on a petition for relief from the claim-filing requirement" and declines to apply the claim presentation laws as rigidly as Golden Gate advocates. (County of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 1303, 1313.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ARIC KECK VS. ODASEVA.COM, INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Atty'S Fees And Costs Under Labor Code Sec 218.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2024, Line 15. DEFENDANT ODASEVA.COM, INC.'s Motion For Atty'S Fees And Costs Under Labor Code Sec 218.5. Defendant's motion for attorney fees and costs is stayed pending resolution of plaintiff's promised appeal. (OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141 [court's inherent power to stay proceedings].) It is this court's practice to address fee/cost issues comprehensibly when litigation is at or near finality, if the parties cannot resolve them. rulings.Case Title: WONG FAMILY BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION VS. SAMUEL WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2024, Line 17. DEFENDANT SAMUEL WONG, WA WONG, AND HUA HUANG's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. Defendants rely solely on Corporations Code 7527's nine-month limitation on "a challenge to an election." (Memo. 2:19-23.) But the FAC does not challenge the at-issue elections' validity; rather, it would uphold the elections. (FAC 6:1-8.) Thus, by the statute's plain language, the nine-month limitation does not apply. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
